meta_title: For Freelancers: user onboarding to Solve can't scale projects (Beginner Case Study)
meta_description: Learn how to user onboarding and overcome can't scale projects. A Beginner-level Case Study using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Freelancers: User Onboarding to Scale SaaS Projects
content.seo_description: Stop struggling with project scaling. Learn how to implement professional user onboarding using Antigravity and Claude Code in this expert-led case study.
content.answer_block: The bottleneck to scaling isn't your code; it's your churn rate. By implementing automated, context-aware user onboarding, you transform passive sign-ups into active users. Use Antigravity to scaffold the UI and Claude Code to iterate on the logic, ensuring every client project ships with a high-conversion, self-service user journey.
content.intro:
You’re drowning in manual support tickets and churned trial users. You’ve mastered the build, but you haven't mastered the retention. Every project you ship feels like a new fire to put out because users don't know how to navigate your interface, leading to the dreaded 'I can't scale' plateau that kills freelance careers.

The secret to breaking through? Stop building features and start building pathways. By automating user onboarding, you turn your chaotic MVP into a guided, professional experience. This isn't just about tooltips; it's about engineering a system that qualifies leads and accelerates time-to-value without you lifting a finger.

steps.title: Define the User Journey Map
steps.description: Before writing code, map out the three 'Aha!' moments. Use AI to structure the onboarding flow to minimize friction.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Act as a Lead Product Designer. Create a JSON schema for a 4-step user onboarding flow that guides a new user through a SaaS dashboard setup, highlighting three core features.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Generate a state machine configuration for an onboarding flow based on the provided JSON schema.'

steps.title: Scaffold the Onboarding Components
steps.description: Generate reusable UI components that can be dropped into any client project.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a React-based onboarding modal component using Tailwind CSS that supports step-by-step navigation and a progress bar.
steps.claude_code_command: claude add 'Create a folder named components/onboarding and populate it with the generated modal logic.'

steps.title: Implement Context-Aware Triggers
steps.description: Ensure the onboarding experience only fires when the user is ready, not immediately upon login.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a TypeScript hook that tracks user completion of onboarding steps and persists the state to local storage.
steps.claude_code_command: claude edit 'Implement the useOnboarding hook into the main App.tsx file.'

steps.title: Integrate Analytics Tracking
steps.description: Measure where users drop off in your onboarding flow to refine your copy.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Add event tracking calls to the onboarding component steps for PostHog or Mixpanel integration.
steps.claude_code_command: claude terminal 'npm install posthog-js' && claude edit 'Integrate tracking events into the onboarding state machine.'

steps.title: Final QA and Stress Testing
steps.description: Use Claude Code to simulate various user paths to ensure the onboarding doesn't break the main app flow.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a suite of Playwright tests to verify the onboarding modal opens and completes correctly across different viewport sizes.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Execute the Playwright test suite and report any UI regressions.'

pro_tips: Never ship onboarding that cannot be skipped; forced tutorials cause immediate user resentment.
Use 'Empty States' as onboarding opportunities—if a user has no data, show a 'Create your first project' call to action.
Keep your onboarding logic decoupled from your main business logic to ensure it can be ported between different client projects easily.
A/B test your first onboarding step's headline; a 10% increase in the first step completion often leads to a 30% increase in long-term retention.
Use session recording (like LogRocket or Hotjar) during your first week of launch to see exactly where users get confused despite your onboarding.
content.next_steps: Once your onboarding flow is live, monitor the drop-off rates for 48 hours. Use the feedback to simplify the text and repeat the process for your next client project.
content.conversion_block: Stop building in isolation and start shipping systems that actually retain users. You have the tools, the strategy, and the workflow. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
